description: HP/Agilent 3458A 8.5-digit Digital Multimeter. Uses its own native command language (not SCPI). Provides DC/AC voltage and current, 2-/4-wire resistance, frequency, period, direct- and sub-sampled digitizing, extensive real-time and post-process math, reading memory, subprograms, state storage, autocalibration and triggering control.

identity.description: Identity Query. The multimeter responds to the ID? command by sending the string 'HP 3458A'.

commands.name: autocal
commands.description: Autocal. Performs an internal self-calibration routine (ALL, DCV, AC, or OHMS) without external standards. Constants are stored in continuous memory.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.range: ALL | DCV | AC | OHMS

parameters.name: security_code
parameters.range: integer (required when autocal secured)

commands.name: ac_bandwidth
commands.description: AC bandwidth. Specifies the frequency content (lowest and highest expected frequency) of the input signal for AC or AC+DC measurements, allowing the fastest possible measurement.

parameters.name: low_frequency
parameters.range: power-on 20 Hz; default 20 Hz

parameters.name: high_frequency
parameters.range: power-on 20 MHz; default 2 MHz

commands.name: gpib_address
commands.description: Sets the multimeter's GPIB address (front panel only). Stored in continuous memory. Address 31 selects Talk Only mode.

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: 0 to 31 (factory setting 22)

commands.name: aperture
commands.description: Aperture. Specifies the A/D converter integration time in seconds, overriding any previously specified integration time or resolution.

parameters.name: aperture
parameters.range: 0 to 1 s in 100 ns increments; values <500 ns select 500 ns minimum; default 500 ns

commands.name: autorange
commands.description: Autorange. Enables or disables the autorange function. Does not operate for direct-/sub-sampled measurements, the TIMER sample event, or the SWEEP command.

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1) | ONCE(2); power-on ON

commands.name: auxiliary_error_query
commands.description: Auxiliary Error Query. Returns the decimal-weighted sum of all set bits in the auxiliary (hardware) error register, then clears the register.

commands.name: autozero
commands.description: Autozero. Enables or disables the autozero function for DC voltage, DC current, and resistance measurements. When ON a zero measurement is subtracted from every reading.

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1) | ONCE(2); power-on ON

commands.name: beeper
commands.description: Controls the multimeter's beeper. When enabled the beeper emits a 1 kHz beep if an error occurs.

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1) | ONCE(2)

commands.name: calibrate
commands.description: Calibration command. Refer to the 3458A Calibration Manual for details.

commands.name: call_subprogram
commands.description: Call Subprogram. Executes a previously stored subprogram created with SUB.

parameters.name: name
parameters.range: alpha/alphanumeric or integer 0-127, up to 10 characters

commands.name: calibration_number_query
commands.description: Calibration Number Query. Returns an integer indicating the number of times the multimeter has been calibrated.

commands.name: calibration_string
commands.description: Calibration String (remote only). Stores a string (up to 75 characters) in the multimeter's nonvolatile calibration RAM.

parameters.name: string
parameters.range: quoted string up to 75 characters

parameters.name: security_code
parameters.range: integer (required when cal RAM secured)

commands.name: compress_subprogram
commands.description: Compress Subprogram. Removes the ASCII text of a stored subprogram to save memory; the subprogram is then lost when power is removed.

parameters.name: name
parameters.range: subprogram name (up to 10 chars or integer 0-127)

commands.name: continue
commands.description: Continue. Resumes execution of a subprogram suspended by a PAUSE command.

commands.name: clear_status_byte
commands.description: Clear Status Byte. Clears (sets to 0) all bits in the status register.

commands.name: defeat
commands.description: Enables or disables the input protection algorithm and some syntax/error checking, allowing faster configuration changes. Use with caution (see manual).

parameters.name: mode
parameters.range: OFF(0)=protection enabled | ON(1)=protection disabled; power-on OFF

commands.name: define_key
commands.description: Define Key. Assigns one or more commands to a user-defined front panel function key (f0-f9). DEFKEY DEFAULT erases all assigned key strings.

parameters.name: string
parameters.range: quoted command list up to 40 characters

commands.name: delay
commands.description: Specifies a time interval inserted between the trigger event and the first sample event.

parameters.name: time
parameters.range: 1E-7 to 6000 s (10 ns increments for sampling, 100 ns otherwise); 0 selects minimum; -1 returns to automatic

commands.name: delete_subprogram
commands.description: Delete Subprogram. Removes a single subprogram from memory.

parameters.name: name
parameters.range: subprogram name (up to 10 chars or integer 0-127)

commands.name: diagnostic
commands.description: Service-related command. Refer to the 3458A Service Manual for details.

commands.name: display
commands.description: Display. Enables or disables the display, sends a message to the display, or clears it.

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1) | MSG(2) | CLR(3)

parameters.name: message
parameters.range: text up to 75 characters

commands.name: direct_sampling
commands.description: Direct-Sampling (digitizing). DSAC measures only the AC component; DSDC measures combined AC+DC. Uses the 2 ns track/hold and 12 MHz input path. No autorange; range set by max._input.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: selects range 10mV..1000V (0 to 1E3); default 10V

parameters.name: %_resolution
parameters.range: ignored (allowed for syntax consistency)

commands.name: error_mask
commands.description: Error Mask. Enables specified error conditions to set the error bit (bit 5) in the status register. Value is the decimal-weighted sum of enabled error conditions.

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: 0 to 32767 (weighted sum of error conditions); power-on 32767

commands.name: eoi_control
commands.description: Enables or disables the GPIB End Or Identify (EOI) function for readings.

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1) | ALWAYS(2)

commands.name: error_query
commands.description: Error Query. Returns the decimal-weighted sum of all set bits in the error register, clears the register, and shuts off the ERR annunciator.

commands.name: error_string_query
commands.description: Error String Query. Reads and clears the least significant set bit in the error or auxiliary error register and returns an error number and explanatory message string.

commands.name: external_output
commands.description: External Output. Specifies the event that generates a signal on the rear panel Ext Out connector and its polarity.

parameters.name: event
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ICOMP(1) | ONCE(2) | APER(3) | BCOMP(4) | SRQ(5) | RCOMP(6); power-on ICOMP

parameters.name: polarity
parameters.range: NEG(0) | POS(1); power-on NEG

commands.name: fixed_impedance
commands.description: Enables or disables fixed input resistance (10 MΩ on all ranges) for DC voltage measurements, preventing input-resistance changes from affecting readings.

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1); power-on OFF

commands.name: frequency
commands.description: Frequency. Measures the frequency of the input signal. Input signal type is set with FSOURCE.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: fixed range value or AUTO; default AUTO

commands.name: frequency_source
commands.description: Frequency Source. Specifies the type of signal used as the input for frequency or period measurements.

parameters.name: source
parameters.range: ACV(2) | ACDCV(3) | ACI(7) | ACDCI(8); power-on ACV

commands.name: function
commands.description: Function. Selects the measurement type and optionally the range and resolution. The FUNC header is optional and may be omitted. Function names (DCV, ACV, OHM, etc.) can be sent directly.

parameters.name: function
parameters.range: DCV(1) | ACV(2) | ACDCV(3) | OHM(4) | OHMF(5) | DCI(6) | ACI(7) | ACDCI(8) | FREQ(9) | PER(10) | DSAC(11) | DSDC(12) | SSAC(13) | SSDC(14); power-on DCV

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: absolute value of expected input to select a range, or AUTO; power-on AUTO

parameters.name: %_resolution
parameters.range: percentage of max._input (or digits/gate for FREQ,PER)

commands.name: dc_voltage
commands.description: Selects DC voltage measurements (FUNC DCV). Ranges 100mV, 1V, 10V, 100V, 1000V.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: 0 to 1E3 selects 100mV..1000V; -1 or AUTO for autorange

parameters.name: %_resolution
parameters.range: percentage of max._input

commands.name: ac_voltage
commands.description: Selects AC voltage measurements (FUNC ACV). Conversion method set by SETACV. Ranges 10mV..1000V.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: 0 to 1E3 selects 10mV..1000V; -1 or AUTO for autorange

parameters.name: %_resolution
parameters.range: percentage of max._input

commands.name: acdc_voltage
commands.description: Selects AC+DC voltage measurements (FUNC ACDCV). Conversion method set by SETACV.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: 0 to 1E3 selects 10mV..1000V; -1 or AUTO for autorange

commands.name: ohms_2wire
commands.description: Selects 2-wire ohms measurements (FUNC OHM). Ranges 10Ω..1GΩ.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: 0 to 1.2E9 selects 10Ω..1GΩ; -1 or AUTO for autorange

parameters.name: %_resolution
parameters.range: percentage of max._input

commands.name: ohms_4wire
commands.description: Selects 4-wire ohms measurements (FUNC OHMF). Ranges 10Ω..1GΩ.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: 0 to 1.2E9 selects 10Ω..1GΩ; -1 or AUTO for autorange

commands.name: dc_current
commands.description: Selects DC current measurements (FUNC DCI). Ranges 0.1uA..1A.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: 0 to 1.2 selects .1uA..1A; -1 or AUTO for autorange

commands.name: ac_current
commands.description: Selects AC current measurements (FUNC ACI). Ranges 100uA..1A.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: 0 to 1.2 selects 100uA..1A; -1 or AUTO for autorange

commands.name: acdc_current
commands.description: Selects AC+DC current measurements (FUNC ACDCI). Ranges 100uA..1A.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: 0 to 1.2 selects 100uA..1A; -1 or AUTO for autorange

commands.name: identity_query
commands.description: Identity Query. The multimeter responds by sending the string 'HP 3458A', allowing the GPIB controller to locate the multimeter by its address.

commands.name: input_buffer
commands.description: Input Buffer. Enables or disables the input buffer. When enabled, commands are stored, releasing the bus immediately.

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1); power-on OFF

commands.name: integer_scale_query
commands.description: Integer Scale Query. Returns the scale factor for readings output in SINT or DINT formats. Multiply integer readings by this factor to restore actual values.

commands.name: level
commands.description: Specifies the level triggering voltage (as a percentage of the present range) and the coupling (AC or DC) for level triggering.

parameters.name: percentage
parameters.range: -500% to +500% (5% steps, sampling) or -120% to 120% (1% steps, DCV); power-on 0%

parameters.name: coupling
parameters.range: DC(1) | AC(2); power-on AC

commands.name: level_filter
commands.description: Level Filter. Enables or disables a single-pole low-pass filter (75 kHz 3-dB point) on the level-detection circuitry to prevent false triggers.

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1); power-on OFF

commands.name: line_frequency_reference
commands.description: Specifies the A/D converter's reference frequency, or with LINE measures the line frequency and sets the reference to the measured value.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 45-65 Hz or 360-440 Hz (divided by 8); or LINE to measure

commands.name: line_frequency_query
commands.description: Line Frequency Query. Measures and returns the frequency of the AC power line.

commands.name: lockout
commands.description: Lockout. Enables or disables the multimeter's front panel keyboard (including the Local key).

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0)=keyboard enabled | ON(1)=keyboard disabled; power-on OFF

commands.name: math
commands.description: Enables or disables real-time math operations (up to two). operation_b is performed on the result of operation_a.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.range: OFF(0) | CONT(1) | CTHRM(3) | DB(4) | DBM(5) | FILTER(6) | FTHRM(8) | NULL(9) | PERC(10) | PFAIL(11) | RMS(12) | SCALE(13) | STAT(14) | CTHRM2K(16) | CTHRM10K(17) | FTHRM2K(18) | FTHRM10K(19) | CRTD85(20) | CRTD92(21) | FRTD85(22) | FRTD92(23); power-on OFF

commands.name: memory_count_query
commands.description: Memory Count Query. Returns the total number of stored readings.

commands.name: memory
commands.description: Memory. Enables or disables reading memory and designates the storage mode (LIFO/FIFO).

parameters.name: mode
parameters.range: OFF(0) | LIFO(1) | FIFO(2) | CONT(3); power-on OFF

commands.name: menu
commands.description: Selects the SHORT or FULL list of commands in the front panel's alphabetic command menu.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.range: SHORT(0) | FULL(1)

commands.name: memory_format
commands.description: Memory Format. Clears reading memory and designates the storage format for new readings.

parameters.name: format
parameters.range: ASCII(1) | SINT(2) | DINT(3) | SREAL(4) | DREAL(5); power-on SREAL

commands.name: memory_math
commands.description: Memory Math. Enables or disables post-process math operations performed as readings are removed/copied from reading memory.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.range: OFF(0) | CONT(1) | CTHRM(3) | DB(4) | DBM(5) | FILTER(6) | FTHRM(8) | NULL(9) | PERC(10) | PFAIL(11) | RMS(12) | SCALE(13) | STAT(14) | CTHRM2K(16) | CTHRM10K(17) | FTHRM2K(18) | FTHRM10K(19) | CRTD85(20) | CRTD92(21) | FRTD85(22) | FRTD92(23); power-on OFF

commands.name: memory_size
commands.description: Memory Size. Accepted for language compatibility (performs no action). The MSIZE? query returns total reading memory and the largest unused block of subprogram/state memory.

commands.name: number_of_digits
commands.description: Number of Digits. Sets the maximum number of digits displayed (implied half digit). Does not affect A/D resolution or readings to memory/bus.

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: 3 to 8 (displays value-and-a-half digits); power-on 7

commands.name: number_of_power_line_cycles
commands.description: Number of Power Line Cycles. Specifies the A/D converter's integration time in power line cycles, primarily to establish normal mode noise rejection.

parameters.name: power_line_cycles
parameters.range: 0-1 PLC (fine steps), 1-10 PLC (1 PLC steps), 10-1000 PLC (10 PLC steps); power-on 10; default 0

commands.name: number_of_readings
commands.description: Number of Readings. Designates the number of readings taken per trigger and the sample event that initiates each reading.

parameters.name: count
parameters.range: 1 to 16777215; power-on 1

parameters.name: event
parameters.range: AUTO(1) | EXTSYN(2) | SYN(5) | TIMER(6) | LEVEL(7) | LINE(8); power-on AUTO

commands.name: offset_compensated_ohms
commands.description: Enables or disables the offset compensated ohms function (10Ω to 100kΩ ranges) for 2-wire and 4-wire resistance measurements.

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1); power-on OFF

commands.name: output_format
commands.description: Output Format. Designates the GPIB output format for readings sent to the controller.

parameters.name: format
parameters.range: ASCII(1) | SINT(2) | DINT(3) | SREAL(4) | DREAL(5); power-on ASCII

commands.name: option_query
commands.description: Option Query. Returns installed options: 0 = none, 1 = Extended Reading Memory Option.

commands.name: pause
commands.description: Suspends subprogram execution. Resume with CONT or GPIB Group Execute Trigger. Allowed only within a subprogram.

commands.name: period
commands.description: Period. Measures the period of the input signal. Input signal type is set with FSOURCE.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: fixed range value or AUTO; default AUTO

commands.name: preset
commands.description: Configures the multimeter to one of three predefined states: NORM (remote operation), FAST (fast readings/transfer), or DIG (DCV digitizing).

parameters.name: type
parameters.range: FAST(0) | NORM(1) | DIG(2); default NORM

commands.name: purge_state
commands.description: Purge State. Removes a single stored state from memory.

parameters.name: name
parameters.range: state name (up to 10 chars or integer 0-127)

commands.name: query_format
commands.description: Query Format. Designates whether query responses contain numeric or alpha characters and whether command headers are returned.

parameters.name: type
parameters.range: NUM(0) | NORM(1) | ALPHA; power-on NORM

commands.name: range_abbrev
commands.description: R is an abbreviation for the RANGE command.

commands.name: range
commands.description: Selects a measurement range or the autorange mode for the present function.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: absolute value of expected input to select a range, or AUTO (-1); power-on AUTO

parameters.name: %_resolution
parameters.range: percentage of max._input (or digits for FREQ/PER)

commands.name: ratio
commands.description: Measures a DC reference voltage on the Sense terminals and a signal voltage on the Input terminals, then computes the ratio (signal/reference).

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1); power-on OFF

commands.name: resolution
commands.description: Resolution. Specifies reading resolution as a percentage of the present range (or digits/gate time for frequency and period).

parameters.name: %_resolution
parameters.range: percentage of range = (actual resolution/range)*100; default determined by integration time

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Sets the multimeter to the power-on state without cycling power. Aborts readings, clears error and status registers (except power-on SRQ), clears reading memory, and executes the default configuration commands.

commands.name: revision_query
commands.description: Revision Query. Returns the master and slave processor firmware revision numbers separated by a comma.

commands.name: recall_math
commands.description: Recall Math. Reads and returns the contents of a math register.

parameters.name: register
parameters.range: DEGREE(1) | LOWER(2) | MAX(3) | MEAN(4) | MIN(5) | NSAMP(6) | OFFSET(7) | PERC(8) | REF(9) | RES(10) | SCALE(11) | SDEV(12) | UPPER(13) | HIRES(14) | PFAILNUM(15); default DEGREE

commands.name: recall_memory
commands.description: Recall Memory. Reads and returns a reading or group of readings stored in reading memory, leaving them intact. Automatically turns reading memory off.

parameters.name: first
parameters.range: beginning reading number; default 1

parameters.name: count
parameters.range: number of readings to recall; default 1

parameters.name: record
parameters.range: record number; default 1

commands.name: request_service
commands.description: Request Service. Enables one or more status register conditions (decimal-weighted sum) to assert the GPIB SRQ line.

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: 0 to 255 (weighted sum of status conditions); power-on 0 or 8

commands.name: recall_state
commands.description: Recall State. Recalls a stored state from memory and configures the multimeter to that state. State 0 holds the power-down state.

parameters.name: name
parameters.range: state name (up to 10 chars or integer 0-127); default 0

commands.name: secure_cal
commands.description: Calibration command. Refer to the 3458A Calibration Manual for details.

commands.name: scratch
commands.description: Clears all subprograms and stored states from memory.

commands.name: security_code
commands.description: Security Code. Enters a security code to prevent unauthorized calibration or autocalibration, and optionally secures autocal. Factory code is 3458.

parameters.name: old_code
parameters.range: previous security code (factory 3458)

parameters.name: new_code
parameters.range: integer -2.1E9 to 2.1E9 (0 disables security)

parameters.name: acal_secure
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1)

commands.name: set_acv
commands.description: Set ACV. Selects the RMS conversion technique (analog, random sampling, or synchronous sampling) for AC or AC+DC voltage measurements.

parameters.name: type
parameters.range: ANA(1) | RNDM(2) | SYNC(3); power-on ANA

commands.name: slope
commands.description: Specifies which slope (positive- or negative-going) of the signal is used by the level-detection circuitry. Used with the LEVEL command.

parameters.name: slope
parameters.range: NEG(0) | POS(1); power-on POS

commands.name: store_math
commands.description: Store Math. Places a number in a math register.

parameters.name: register
parameters.range: DEGREE(1) | LOWER(2) | MAX(3) | MEAN(4) | MIN(5) | NSAMP(6) | OFFSET(7) | PERC(8) | REF(9) | RES(10) | SCALE(11) | UPPER(13) | HIRES(14) | PFAILNUM(15); default DEGREE

parameters.name: number
parameters.range: value to place in register; default last reading

commands.name: service_request
commands.description: Service Request. Sets bit 2 in the status register. If enabled (RQS 4), asserts the GPIB SRQ line.

commands.name: sub_sampling
commands.description: Sub-Sampling (digitizing of periodic signals). SSAC measures only the AC component; SSDC measures combined AC+DC. No autorange/autozero; range set by max._input. Interval and sample count set by SWEEP.

parameters.name: max._input
parameters.range: selects range 10mV..1000V (0 to 1E3); default 10V

parameters.name: %_resolution
parameters.range: ignored (allowed for syntax consistency)

commands.name: sub_sampling_parameters_query
commands.description: Sub-Sampling Parameters Query. Returns the parameters needed to reconstruct a sub-sampled waveform when samples are sent directly to the GPIB output buffer.

commands.name: sync_source
commands.description: Sync Source. Synchronizes sub-sampling or synchronous ACV/ACDCV sampling to an external signal or to a voltage level on the input signal.

parameters.name: source
parameters.range: EXT(2) | LEVEL(7); power-on LEVEL

parameters.name: mode
parameters.range: AUTO(1) | HOLD(4); power-on AUTO (synchronous AC only)

commands.name: store_state
commands.description: Store State. Stores the multimeter's present state and assigns it a name. States are recalled with RSTATE.

parameters.name: name
parameters.range: state name (up to 10 chars or integer 0-127); integer names get STATE prefix

commands.name: status_byte_query
commands.description: Status Byte Query. Returns the decimal-weighted sum of all set bits in the status register.

commands.name: subprogram
commands.description: Subprogram. Stores a series of commands as a named subprogram. Terminated by SUBEND. A subprogram named 0 is the autostart subprogram.

parameters.name: name
parameters.range: alpha/alphanumeric or integer 0-127, up to 10 characters

commands.name: subprogram_end
commands.description: Subprogram End. Signals the end of a subprogram. When executed, sets bit 1 (if enabled) in the status register to signal completion.

commands.name: sweep
commands.description: Specifies the effective interval between samples and the total number of samples taken per trigger event (or per trigger arm event for sub-sampling). Replaces NRDGS n,TIMER and TIMER.

parameters.name: effective_interval
parameters.range: 10E-9 to 6000 s (sub-sampling) or 1/max-rate to 6000 s; power-on 100E-9, default 20us

parameters.name: #_samples
parameters.range: 1 to 1.67E7; power-on/default 1024

commands.name: trig_abbrev
commands.description: T is an abbreviation for the TRIG command.

commands.name: trigger_arm
commands.description: Trigger Arm. Defines the event that enables (arms) the trigger event. Can also perform multiple measurement cycles via number_arms with the SGL event.

parameters.name: event
parameters.range: AUTO(1) | EXT(2) | SGL(3) | HOLD(4) | SYN(5); power-on AUTO

parameters.name: number_arms
parameters.range: 0 to 2.1E9 (valid only with SGL); default 1

commands.name: trigger_buffer
commands.description: Trigger Buffer. Enables or disables the external trigger buffer. ON corrects for TRIGGER TOO FAST errors with external triggers.

parameters.name: control
parameters.range: OFF(0) | ON(1); power-on OFF

commands.name: temperature_query
commands.description: Temperature Query. Returns the multimeter's internal temperature in degrees Centigrade.

commands.name: terminals
commands.description: Accepted for language compatibility (input terminals cannot be controlled from remote). The TERM? query returns which terminals (FRONT or REAR) are selected by the front panel switch.

parameters.name: source
parameters.range: OPEN(0) | FRONT(1) | REAR(2)

commands.name: self_test
commands.description: Causes the multimeter to perform a series of internal self-tests. Disconnect input signals before running. Errors set bits in the error/auxiliary error registers.

commands.name: timer
commands.description: Defines the time interval for the TIMER sample event in the NRDGS command. The interval is inserted between readings.

parameters.name: time
parameters.range: 1/max-rate to 6000 s in 100 ns increments; power-on/default 1 s

commands.name: tone
commands.description: Causes the multimeter to beep once, then returns to the previous BEEP mode.

commands.name: trigger
commands.description: Specifies the trigger event. Operates with the trigger arm event (TARM) and sample event (NRDGS). Ignored for sub-sampling.

parameters.name: event
parameters.range: AUTO(1) | EXT(2) | SGL(3) | HOLD(4) | SYN(5) | LEVEL(7) | LINE(8); power-on AUTO, default SGL
